---
title: "Plots"
output: html_document
---

# Packages Used
```{r}
library(tidyverse)
```

# Variance Plot
```{r}
# Color Palette
okabe_ito <- c("#E69F00", "#56B4E9", "#009E73", "#F0E442", "#0072B2", "#D55E00",
  "#CC79A7", "#999999")

# Read and combine results, add 95% Monte Carlo intervals
var_results <- var_results <- c(
  "homogeneous" = "homo_data.csv",
  "heterogeneous-mean" = "hetero_mean_data.csv",
  "heterogeneous-block" = "hetero_beta_data.csv"
  ) |>
  map_dfr(read_csv, .id = "variance") |>
  mutate(
    dataset = factor(
      dataset,
      levels = c(
        "normal",
        "lognormal",
        "log_lognormal",
        "exponential"
      ),
      labels = c(
        "Normal",
        "Lognormal",
        "Log-lognormal",
        "Exponential"
      )
    ),
    variance = factor(
      variance,
      levels = c(
        "homogeneous",
        "heterogeneous-mean",
        "heterogeneous-block"
      ),
      labels = c(
        "Homogeneous",
        "Heterogeneous mean",
        "Heterogeneous block"
      )
    ),
    mcse = sqrt(type1_error * (1 - type1_error) / 10000),
    lower = pmax(0, type1_error - 1.96 * mcse),
    upper = pmin(1, type1_error + 1.96 * mcse)
  )

pd <- position_dodge(width = 0.45)

# Plot
p_variance <- ggplot(
  var_results,
  aes(
    x = dataset,
    y = type1_error,
    color = variance,
    shape = variance
  )
) +
  geom_hline(
    yintercept = 0.05,
    linetype = "dashed",
    linewidth = 0.7
  ) +
  geom_errorbar(
    aes(ymin = lower, ymax = upper),
    position = pd,
    width = 0.12,
    linewidth = 0.5
  ) +
  geom_point(
    position = pd,
    size = 3
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    limits = c(0, 0.06),
    breaks = seq(0, 0.06, 0.01),
    labels = scales::label_percent(accuracy = 1),
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.02))
  ) +
  scale_color_manual(
    values = okabe_ito[1:3]
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Empirical Type I Error of RCBD ANOVA Under Heteroskedasticity",
    subtitle = "Dashed line indicates the nominal α = 0.05 level",
    x = "Error distribution",
    y = "Empirical Type I error rate",
    color = "Variance condition",
    shape = "Variance condition"
  ) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 11) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 10),
    legend.position = "bottom",
    legend.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    axis.text.x = element_text(margin = margin(t = 5))
  )

p_variance
```

# Outlier Plot
```{r}
# Read and combine results
outlier_results <- c(
  "Normal outliers" = "normal_outlier_data.csv",
  "Constant outliers" = "constant_outlier_data.csv",
  "Cauchy outliers" = "cauchy_outlier_data.csv"
) |>
  map_dfr(read_csv, .id = "outlier_condition") |>
  mutate(
    outlier_condition = factor(
      outlier_condition,
      levels = c(
        "Normal outliers",
        "Constant outliers",
        "Cauchy outliers"
      )
    )
  )

# Add 95% Monte Carlo intervals
outlier_results <- outlier_results |>
  mutate(
    mcse = sqrt(type1_error * (1 - type1_error) / 10000),
    lower = pmax(0, type1_error - 1.96 * mcse),
    upper = pmin(1, type1_error + 1.96 * mcse)
  )

pd <- position_dodge(width = 0.003)

# Plot
p_outliers <- ggplot(
  outlier_results,
  aes(
    x = pi,
    y = type1_error,
    color = outlier_condition,
    shape = outlier_condition,
    group = outlier_condition
  )
) +
  geom_hline(
    yintercept = 0.05,
    linetype = "dashed",
    linewidth = 0.7
  ) +
  geom_errorbar(
    aes(ymin = lower, ymax = upper),
    width = 0.0025,
    linewidth = 0.4,
    alpha = 0.7
  ) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.7) +
  geom_point(size = 2.6) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks = seq(0, 0.20, 0.05),
    labels = scales::label_percent(accuracy = 1),
    expand = expansion(add = 0.004)
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    breaks = seq(0, 0.06, 0.01),
    labels = scales::label_percent(accuracy = 1),
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.02))
  ) +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0, 0.06)) +
  scale_color_manual(values = okabe_ito[1:3]) +
  labs(
    title = "Empirical Type I Error of RCBD ANOVA Under Outliers",
    subtitle = "Dashed line indicates the nominal α = 0.05 level",
    x = "Proportion of outliers",
    y = "Empirical Type I error rate",
    color = "Outlier condition",
    shape = "Outlier condition"
  ) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 11) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 10),
    legend.position = "bottom",
    legend.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    axis.text.x = element_text(margin = margin(t = 5))
  )

p_outliers
```

# Missing Data Plot

```{r}
# Read and clean results, add Monte Carlo intervals
missing_results <- read_csv("missing_data.csv") |>
  mutate(
    method = factor(
      method,
      levels = c(
        "complete",
        "CMIB",
        "mean_imp",
        "CMIT",
        "linear_imp"
      ),
      labels = c(
        "Complete data",
        "CMIB",
        "Mean imputation",
        "CMIT",
        "Linear imputation"
      )
    ),
    type1_mcse = sqrt(type1_error * (1 - type1_error) / 10000),
    type1_lower = pmax(0, type1_error - 1.96 * type1_mcse),
    type1_upper = pmin(1, type1_error + 1.96 * type1_mcse),

    F_mcse = sqrt(var_F / 10000),
    F_lower = mean_F - 1.96 * F_mcse,
    F_upper = mean_F + 1.96 * F_mcse
  )

f_scale <- 5
pd <- position_dodge(width = 0.35)

# Plot
p_missing <- ggplot(
  missing_results,
  aes(x = method)
  ) +
  geom_hline(
    yintercept = 0.05,
    linetype = "dashed",
    linewidth = 0.7
  ) +
  geom_hline(
    yintercept = 1.05 / f_scale,
    linetype = "dotted",
    linewidth = 0.7,
    color = okabe_ito[2]
  ) +
  geom_errorbar(
    aes(
      ymin = type1_lower,
      ymax = type1_upper,
      color = "Type I error"
    ),
    position = pd,
    width = 0.12,
    linewidth = 0.5
  ) +
  geom_point(
    aes(
      y = type1_error,
      color = "Type I error",
      shape = "Type I error"
    ),
    position = pd,
    size = 3
  ) +
  geom_errorbar(
    aes(
      ymin = F_lower / f_scale,
      ymax = F_upper / f_scale,
      color = "Mean F-statistic"
    ),
    position = pd,
    width = 0.12,
    linewidth = 0.5
  ) +
  geom_point(
    aes(
      y = mean_F / f_scale,
      color = "Mean F-statistic",
      shape = "Mean F-statistic"
    ),
    position = pd,
    size = 3
  ) +

  scale_y_continuous(
    name = "Empirical Type I error rate",
    breaks = seq(0, 0.50, 0.10),
    labels = scales::label_percent(accuracy = 1),
    sec.axis = sec_axis(
      transform = ~ . * f_scale,
      name = "Mean F-statistic",
      breaks = seq(0, 2.5, 0.5)
    ),
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.03))
  ) +

  coord_cartesian(
    ylim = c(0, 0.50)
  ) +

  scale_color_manual(
    values = c(
      "Type I error" = okabe_ito[1],
      "Mean F-statistic" = okabe_ito[2]
    )
  ) +

  scale_shape_manual(
    values = c(
      "Type I error" = 16,
      "Mean F-statistic" = 18
    )
  ) +

  labs(
    title = "Type I Error and Mean F-statistic With Missing Data",
    subtitle = paste(
      "Dashed line: nominal α = 0.05;",
      "dotted line: expected null mean F = 1.05"
    ),
    x = "Missing-data method",
    color = NULL,
    shape = NULL
  ) +

  theme_classic(base_size = 11) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 10),
    legend.position = "bottom",

    axis.text.x = element_text(
      angle = 25,
      hjust = 1,
      margin = margin(t = 5)
    )
  )

p_missing
```
